expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-planner
expectations.evidence: Step 1 in transcript: 'Tool: Read, File: skills/playwright-test-planner/SKILL.md, Result: Successfully read the skill file.'

expectations.text: Mentions planner_setup_page for browser initialization
expectations.evidence: Step 1 lists 'Initialize with planner_setup_page' as the first workflow step, and Step 6 shows an actual attempt to call mcp__playwright-test__planner_setup_page (which was denied due to permissions).

expectations.text: Mentions browser_snapshot for page structure exploration
expectations.evidence: Step 1 states: 'Explore systematically with browser_snapshot, browser_click, browser_type, browser_navigate'. However, browser_snapshot was never actually invoked -- the agent fell back to static code analysis after planner_setup_page was denied.

expectations.text: Describes user flow mapping with happy path and edge cases
expectations.evidence: The test plan covers happy path flows (sections 1-4: page loading, link navigation, layout/styling, accessibility) and edge cases (section 5: '5.1 존재하지 않는 경로 접근' for 404 handling, '5.2 정적 에셋 로딩 실패 시 동작' for asset loading failures).

expectations.text: Each scenario includes steps, expected outcomes, and success criteria
expectations.evidence: All 14 scenarios consistently include '단계' (steps), '기대 결과' (expected outcomes), '성공 기준' (success criteria), and '실패 조건' (failure conditions). For example, scenario 1.1 has numbered steps, bullet-pointed expected results, and explicit success/failure criteria.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_save_plan to save specs/ markdown
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly states in Limitations: 'The planner_save_plan tool was not used; the plan was saved directly as a markdown file.' The plan was written to outputs/test-plan.md, not to a specs/ directory. While planner_save_plan is mentioned in Step 1 as part of reading the SKILL.md workflow, it was never actually used to save anything.

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-planner
expectations.evidence: Step 1 explicitly states: '도구: Read, 파일: skills/playwright-test-planner/SKILL.md' and summarizes the workflow read from it including the 5-step process.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_setup_page for browser initialization
expectations.evidence: Step 1 notes 'planner_setup_page로 브라우저 초기화' from the SKILL.md workflow. Step 5 loads it via ToolSearch. Step 6 attempts to call mcp__playwright-test__planner_setup_page (permission denied).

expectations.text: Includes authentication as a prerequisite step
expectations.evidence: Multiple test scenarios (3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 4.1, 4.2) begin with '관리자로 로그인한다' (log in as admin) as a prerequisite step. Suite 1 covers the login flow itself. The entire plan is structured around authentication as a gate to admin access.

expectations.text: Describes authorized vs unauthorized access scenarios
expectations.evidence: Suite 2 '인증 보호 (접근 제어)' covers unauthorized access: 2.1 unauthenticated dashboard access, 2.2 unauthenticated sub-page access, 2.3 expired session access. Suites 3-4 cover authorized scenarios (dashboard rendering, navigation, logout) that require prior login.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_save_plan to save specs/ markdown
expectations.evidence: Step 1 lists planner_save_plan as part of the SKILL.md workflow ('planner_save_plan으로 저장'). Step 5 loads it via ToolSearch. Step 9 explicitly attempts to call mcp__playwright-test__planner_save_plan but receives permission denied. The tool is clearly mentioned and attempted.

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-planner
expectations.evidence: Step 1 in transcript: 'Tool: Read, File: ...\skills\playwright-test-planner\SKILL.md, Result: Successfully read the skill definition. The skill is playwright-test-planner...'

expectations.text: Mentions planner_setup_page for browser initialization
expectations.evidence: Step 1 lists 'Initialize with planner_setup_page' in the workflow. Step 5 loads the tool via ToolSearch. Step 7 attempts to call mcp__playwright-test__planner_setup_page (permission denied).

expectations.text: Covers Create, Read, Update, Delete operations separately
expectations.evidence: Output plan has separate numbered sections: '2. 할 일 생성 (Create)' with 4 scenarios, '4. 할 일 조회 (Read)' with 2 scenarios, '5. 할 일 수정 (Update)' with 3 scenarios, '6. 할 일 삭제 (Delete)' with 3 scenarios.

expectations.text: Includes form validation edge cases (empty input, special characters, max length)
expectations.evidence: Suite 3 covers: 3.1 empty input ('빈 입력으로 할 일 추가 시도'), 3.2 whitespace-only ('공백만 있는 입력'), 3.4 long text ('300자 이상의 매우 긴 텍스트'). Special characters covered in 2.4 with XSS test ('<script>alert(1)</script>').

expectations.text: Includes empty state scenario (no items in list)
expectations.evidence: Suite 1 '빈 상태 처리' has two scenarios: 1.1 '초기 빈 상태 표시 확인' (initial empty state) and 1.2 '모든 할 일 삭제 후 빈 상태 복귀' (empty state after deleting all items).

expectations.text: Scenarios are independent and ordered with numbered headings
expectations.evidence: Six numbered top-level headings (### 1 through ### 6), each with numbered sub-scenarios (1.1, 1.2, 2.1-2.4, etc.). Each scenario specifies its own spec file path and starts from a clean state.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_save_plan to save specs/ markdown
expectations.evidence: Step 1 lists 'Save the plan with planner_save_plan' in the workflow. Step 8 attempts mcp__playwright-test__planner_save_plan with the full test plan content. The tool was mentioned and invoked, though it failed due to permission restrictions, and the fallback saved to outputs/ instead of specs/.

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-planner
expectations.evidence: This is a 'without_skill' run. The transcript shows no mention of reading any SKILL.md file. The agent read package.json, page.tsx, layout.tsx, next.config.ts, and globals.css — all application files, no skill instructions.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_setup_page for browser initialization
expectations.evidence: No mention of planner_setup_page anywhere in the transcript or outputs. The transcript explicitly notes: 'No browser exploration was performed (e.g., no Playwright MCP tools used) -- the analysis was based entirely on static code reading.'

expectations.text: Mentions browser_snapshot for page structure exploration
expectations.evidence: No mention of browser_snapshot in the transcript or outputs. The Observations section confirms: 'No browser exploration was performed... the analysis was based entirely on static code reading.'

expectations.text: Describes user flow mapping with happy path and edge cases
expectations.evidence: The test plan contains 15 individual test cases (TC-01 through TC-15) organized as element-level checks (logo visibility, heading text, link verification, etc.). There is no framing of 'user flows,' no 'happy path' designation, and no 'edge cases' section. The tests are static property assertions, not flow-based scenarios.

expectations.text: Each scenario includes steps, expected outcomes, and success criteria
expectations.evidence: Each of the 15 test cases includes a 'Steps' section with numbered steps and an 'Expected' section describing the success condition. For example, TC-01 has 4 steps and 'Expected: Page loads without errors, title and lang attribute are correct.' While 'success criteria' is not a separately labeled section, the 'Expected' field serves this role for every test case.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_save_plan to save specs/ markdown
expectations.evidence: No mention of planner_save_plan anywhere in the transcript or outputs. The output was saved via the Write tool to 'outputs/test-plan.md', not to a specs/ directory, and without using any planner-specific MCP tool.

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-planner
expectations.evidence: This is a 'without_skill' run. The transcript shows only Read calls for package.json, page.tsx, and layout.tsx. No SKILL.md file was read. The executor had no awareness of the playwright-test-planner skill.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_setup_page for browser initialization
expectations.evidence: No mention of planner_setup_page anywhere in the transcript or outputs. The transcript explicitly states: 'No Playwright-specific tools (browser, planner, etc.) were used since the task was to create a plan, not execute tests.'

expectations.text: Includes authentication as a prerequisite step
expectations.evidence: The test plan includes authentication as a prerequisite throughout. The Playwright Implementation Notes section describes using 'storageState' pattern with 'global-setup.ts' that 'logs in and saves the authentication state.' Test Suite 2 tests require clearing cookies as a first step, establishing auth state as a precondition.

expectations.text: Describes authorized vs unauthorized access scenarios
expectations.evidence: Test Suite 2 (Authentication Guard) covers both: TC-2.1/2.2/2.3 test unauthenticated users being redirected from /admin, /admin/users, /admin/settings; TC-2.4/2.5/2.6 test authenticated users successfully accessing those same pages. TC-4.1 additionally tests non-admin role being denied access.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_save_plan to save specs/ markdown
expectations.evidence: No mention of planner_save_plan anywhere in the transcript or outputs. The test plan was saved directly as 'test-plan.md' using the Write tool, not via the planner_save_plan tool and not to a specs/ directory.

expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-planner
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows no mention of reading SKILL.md. The agent read only 'features/next-app/package.json' and 'features/next-app/app/page.tsx'. This is a without_skill run, so the agent had no awareness of SKILL.md.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_setup_page for browser initialization
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'planner_setup_page' anywhere in the transcript or the test-plan.md output. The agent did not reference any MCP browser tools.

expectations.text: Covers Create, Read, Update, Delete operations separately
expectations.evidence: The test-plan.md has distinct numbered sections: '### 2. Create (Add Todo)' with 3 scenarios, '### 3. Read (Display Todos)' with 2 scenarios, '### 4. Update (Edit Todo)' with 4 scenarios, '### 5. Delete (Remove Todo)' with 2 scenarios.

expectations.text: Includes form validation edge cases (empty input, special characters, max length)
expectations.evidence: Section 6 'Form Validation' includes: Scenario 6.1 'Reject empty submission', Scenario 6.4 'Validate maximum character length (if applicable)'. Section 7 includes Scenario 7.2 'Special characters in todo text' with XSS example. All three edge case types are present.

expectations.text: Includes empty state scenario (no items in list)
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Empty State (Initial Load)' includes Scenario 1.1 'Display empty state on first visit' (no todos saved, empty state message displayed) and Scenario 1.2 'Return to empty state after deleting all todos'.

expectations.text: Scenarios are independent and ordered with numbered headings
expectations.evidence: Scenarios are organized under numbered headings (1 through 7) with sub-numbering (1.1, 1.2, 2.1, etc.). Each scenario has its own Given/When/Then block and can be understood independently.

expectations.text: Mentions planner_save_plan to save specs/ markdown
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'planner_save_plan' tool anywhere in the transcript or outputs. The agent used a plain 'Write' tool to save 'test-plan.md' directly in the outputs directory, not via the planner_save_plan MCP tool and not into a 'specs/' directory.
